title: phlegmariurus tetrastichoides
intro: Phlegmariurus tetrastichoides, also known as the four-angled forked polypody, is a species of fern in the family Lycopodiaceae. It is a small, scrambling plant with forked stems and leaves, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. Preparations of Phlegmariurus tetrastichoides are used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and are often made into teas, infusions, or dried extracts for consumption.
